title: Raycaster for Click Detection
prompt:
# TASK 1: Raycaster for Click Detection

## CONTEXT
Phase 4: Selection & Measure. Need to detect when user clicks on the 3D model.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Set up Three.js Raycaster in renderer.js
2. On mouse click in viewport, cast ray from camera through mouse position
3. Detect intersection with current mesh
4. If hit, log the intersection point (x, y, z) to console
5. Ignore clicks when no mesh loaded
6. Ignore clicks on grid/axes (only detect mesh)

## NOTES
- Use Raycaster and mouse position normalized to (-1, 1)
- Only respond to left-click
- Don't interfere with OrbitControls drag

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. npm start
2. Generate a model
3. Click on model surface
4. Console logs intersection coordinates

Provide a plan before implementing code.

title: Capture Surface Normal at Click Point
prompt:
# TASK 2: Capture Surface Normal at Click Point

## CONTEXT
When user clicks on model, we need both position AND surface normal for Claude to understand what face was clicked.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Extend click detection to also capture face normal
2. Raycaster intersection includes face normal
3. Log both position and normal on click
4. Store last click info: { position: {x,y,z}, normal: {x,y,z} }

## NOTES
- intersection.face.normal gives local normal
- May need to transform to world coordinates
- Normal helps Claude understand orientation (top face vs side face)

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. Click on top of a box
2. Console shows position AND normal (0,0,1) indicating Z-up face
3. Click on side, normal shows (1,0,0) or similar

Provide a plan before implementing code.

title: Inject Click Coordinates into Prompts
prompt:
# TASK 3: Inject Click Coordinates into Prompts

## CONTEXT
When user clicks then types, Claude should know where they clicked.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Store last click info in renderer state
2. When sending chat message, include click info if recent (within last 30 seconds)
3. Add to prompt: "User clicked at position (x, y, z) on surface with normal (nx, ny, nz)"
4. Clear click info after it's been used in a prompt
5. Update claude-manager.js buildPrompt to accept optional click data

## NOTES
- Click info passed via IPC with chat message
- Format coordinates to 1 decimal place for readability
- Only include if user clicked recently before typing

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. Click on model surface
2. Type "fillet this edge"
3. Prompt to Claude includes click coordinates
4. Claude interprets location and modifies appropriately

Provide a plan before implementing code.

title: Confirmation Step Before Modification
prompt:
# TASK 4: Confirmation Step Before Modification

## CONTEXT
Before modifying geometry based on click selection, ask user to confirm Claude understood correctly.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. When Claude responds to a click-based request, show confirmation dialog
2. Dialog shows: "Claude wants to: [action description]. Proceed?"
3. Extract action description from Claude's explanation
4. [Yes] button executes the code
5. [No] button cancels, user can rephrase
6. Skip confirmation for non-click-based requests

## NOTES
- Simple modal dialog in renderer
- Only trigger when click coordinates were included in prompt
- Keep previous mesh until confirmed

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. Click on edge, type "fillet this"
2. Dialog: "Claude wants to: Add 2mm fillet to the selected edge. Proceed?"
3. Click Yes -> model updates
4. Click No -> nothing changes, can retry

Provide a plan before implementing code.

title: Highlight Model on Hover
prompt:
# TASK 5: Highlight Model on Hover

## CONTEXT
Visual feedback when hovering over clickable geometry.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. On mouse move, raycast to detect hover over mesh
2. When hovering over mesh, show visual highlight
3. For now, highlight entire model (not individual faces)
4. Highlight options: outline glow, color tint, or emissive boost
5. Remove highlight when mouse leaves mesh
6. Don't highlight during drag (OrbitControls active)

## NOTES
- Keep it subtle - don't obscure the model
- Could use OutlinePass from postprocessing, or simple emissive change
- Performance matters - don't raycast every frame, throttle to ~30fps

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. Hover mouse over model -> subtle highlight appears
2. Move mouse away -> highlight disappears
3. Drag to rotate -> no highlight flicker

Provide a plan before implementing code.

title: Measure Tool - Two Click Distance
prompt:
# TASK 6: Measure Tool - Two Click Distance

## CONTEXT
Users need to measure distances on their models.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. Add measure mode toggle (button or M key)
2. In measure mode, first click sets point A
3. Second click sets point B
4. Display distance between A and B in mm
5. Show visual line between the two points
6. Show measurement label near midpoint
7. Clear measurement on next action or Escape

## NOTES
- Use Three.js Line for visual connection
- Use CSS2D or sprite for distance label
- Exit measure mode after measurement shown
- Round to 1 decimal place

## EXPECTED RESULT
1. Enter measure mode
2. Click point A on model
3. Click point B on model
4. See line between points with label "23.5 mm"
5. Press Escape or click elsewhere to clear

Provide a plan before implementing code.
